name: Sugriva
description: Sugriva is a prominent figure in the Hindu epic, the Ramayana. He is the monkey king of Kishkindha, and the younger brother of Vali. Sugriva's narrative is intricately linked with Rama's quest to rescue Sita. He is known for his strength, agility, and his crucial role in forming an alliance with Rama. Driven from his kingdom by Vali due to a misunderstanding and false accusations, Sugriva sought refuge on Rishyamukha mountain, where Vali was forbidden to tread. His alliance with Rama was forged through Hanuman, and it became instrumental in Rama's eventual victory over Ravana. Sugriva promised Rama assistance in finding Sita in exchange for Rama's help in reclaiming his kingdom. The resulting battle between Rama and Vali, in which Rama ultimately kills Vali, solidifies the bond between Rama and Sugriva, paving the way for the Vanara army's crucial support in the war against Ravana in Lanka. Sugriva's reign after Vali's death is marked by his loyalty to Rama and his commitment to the cause.
subtitle: Monkey King of Kishkindha

keyMyths.title: The Exile of Sugriva
keyMyths.description: Sugriva was unjustly banished from Kishkindha by his elder brother, Vali, due to a misunderstanding regarding a demon in a cave. Vali believed Sugriva had betrayed him and attempted to seize the kingdom. This exile led Sugriva to seek refuge on Rishyamukha Mountain, where Vali was forbidden to enter due to a curse. This event sets the stage for Sugriva's alliance with Rama, who helps him regain his kingdom.

keyMyths.title: Sugriva's Alliance with Rama
keyMyths.description: Sugriva, through the mediation of Hanuman, formed an alliance with Rama. Sugriva promised to help Rama find Sita, who had been abducted by Ravana, in exchange for Rama's assistance in defeating Vali and reclaiming Kishkindha. This alliance was a pivotal moment in the Ramayana, as it brought the strength of the Vanara army to Rama's side, significantly bolstering his chances of success against Ravana.

keyMyths.title: The Duel Between Vali and Sugriva
keyMyths.description: Rama, fulfilling his promise to Sugriva, intervened in the duel between Vali and Sugriva. Vali, empowered by a boon that allowed him to absorb half the strength of his opponent, was initially dominant. However, Rama, hidden behind the trees, shot Vali with an arrow, leading to Vali's death. This event restored Sugriva to the throne of Kishkindha and cemented the alliance between Rama and the Vanara army.

keyMyths.title: Sugriva's Contribution to the War in Lanka
keyMyths.description: After reclaiming his kingdom, Sugriva honored his commitment to Rama by dispatching his vast Vanara army, led by Hanuman, Angada, and Jambavan, to assist in the war against Ravana in Lanka. The Vanara army played a crucial role in building the bridge to Lanka (Rama Setu) and in the subsequent battles against Ravana's forces, proving instrumental in Rama's ultimate victory.

sources.author: Valmiki
